app.title: Project Hub
projects.runningBar.label: Running ({count}):
projects.column.name: Name
projects.filter.name: Filter by name
projects.empty.title: No repositories
projects.empty.message: Bitbucket workspace returned an empty list.
projects.noMatches.title: No matches
projects.noMatches.message: Active filters narrowed the list to zero results. Clear them to see all {count} repos again.
projects.noStarred.title: No starred projects yet
projects.noStarred.message: Click the star icon next to any project's slug to pin it here. Useful when you regularly work with a few of the hundred-plus repos.
projects.error.title: Couldn't load projects
projects.row.taskCount: {count} open Jira tasks assigned to you
tasks.subtitle: Tasks across all your Jira projects
tasks.empty.message: Either you don't have any open issues assigned, or Jira credentials are not configured.
tasks.empty.noMatches.title: No matches
tasks.empty.noMatches.message: Filter narrowed the list to zero. Adjust the search box.
tasks.error.title: Couldn't load tasks
tasks.assigneePicker.noAssignable: No assignable users for this issue.
tasks.mismatch.tooltip: Mentions {slugs} — different from the Jira project this task lives in
tasks.projectTab.noMatch.message: No Jira project matches this slug. Looked for a project whose name starts with {slug} (case-insensitive).
tasks.projectTab.noMatch.hint: If a project does exist, rename it to start with the slug, or check that Jira credentials are configured (Settings → Jira).
tasks.projectTab.noOpenInProject: No open tasks in this project.
tasks.projectTab.closedHidden: {count} closed task(s) hidden — expand "Recently done" above to see them.
drawer.notFound.title: Project not found
drawer.notFound.message: {slug} is not in the current Bitbucket workspace list.
drawer.action.pullDisabled.running: Stop the running process before pulling
drawer.action.deleteDisabled.running: Stop the running process before deleting
drawer.deleteDialog.title: Delete local clone of {slug}?
drawer.deleteDialog.description: This removes the cloned folder at {path} and clears any per-project run / database overrides. The project stays in the source list and the database is untouched — you can re-setup any time.
drawer.checklist.cantDetectRunnable: ⚠️ Cannot detect runnable project. Set workingDirSubpath override in Settings.
drawer.branch.label: Branch:
drawer.commits.noDiffstat: No diffstat available (often the case for the initial commit).
drawer.commits.diffEmpty: Empty diff (binary file or no textual changes).
drawer.pipelines.scopeHint: If pipelines run on this repo but the list is empty — the API token may be missing the read:pipeline:bitbucket scope. Recreate the token with that scope added.
drawer.pipelines.stepLogPending: Logs available once the step finishes
drawer.pipelines.emptyLog: No log output for this step.
drawer.tab.errorState.message: Could not load — check Bitbucket credentials in Settings.
drawer.lastCommit.title: Recent commits
drawer.runOverride.title: Run override
drawer.runOverride.runCommand.hint: Leave empty to use the default from Settings → Defaults.
drawer.runOverride.cwd.hint: Where the run command executes. Empty means project root, or auto-detected for .NET projects.
drawer.runOverride.detect.hint: Inspect the cloned project and propose a run command + working directory based on its files (.csproj / package.json / Cargo.toml / go.mod / Makefile).
drawer.dbOverride.title: Database override
drawer.dbOverride.connection.hint: Which DB connection from Settings → Databases this project uses. Default = first connection.
drawer.dbOverride.name: Database name
drawer.dbOverride.name.hint: DB name on the engine. Empty = slug in lowercase. Override if your project's DB is named differently. Type to filter or pick from existing databases on the engine.
drawer.dbOverride.detect.hint: Scan all configured DB connections for one that already has a database matching this project's slug.
drawer.notes.title: Notes
drawer.notes.localOnly: Stored locally on this machine; not synced.
drawer.dropDialog.title: Drop database {name}?
drawer.dropDialog.description: This will permanently delete all data in the database. This action cannot be undone.
drawer.replaceDialog.title: Replace database {name}?
drawer.replaceDialog.description: Database currently has {size} of data. Restoring will DROP and recreate it from {file}. Existing data will be permanently lost.
drawer.db.configureFirst: Configure database connection in Settings to enable DB actions.
settings.title: Settings
settings.defaults.title: Defaults
settings.defaults.description: Default run command for new projects. Each project can override it from its drawer.
settings.defaults.runCommand.hint: Full command line — 'dotnet run' / 'npm run dev' / 'go run .' / etc. Parsed with shell-like quoting; cwd is the project root unless overridden per project.
settings.databases.title: Databases
settings.databases.description: Local or remote DB connections used for size detection, dump restore, and per-project DB ops.
settings.databases.empty: No databases configured. Add one to enable DB-aware features.
settings.databases.name: Display name
settings.databases.name.hint: Free-form label shown in pickers and elsewhere.
settings.databases.confirmRemove: Remove this database? Stored password will be cleared.
settings.databases.confirmRemove.title: Remove database {name}?
settings.sources.title: Sources
settings.sources.description: Repository sources. Each source is one VCS workspace or org; the projects list merges repos from all of them.
settings.sources.empty: No sources configured. Add one to start listing projects.
settings.sources.name: Display name
settings.sources.name.hint: Free-form label shown in the projects list and elsewhere.
settings.sources.confirmRemove: Remove this source? Stored token will be cleared.
settings.sources.confirmRemove.title: Remove source {name}?
settings.github.owner.hint: GitHub user or organization slug (the part after github.com/).
settings.github.gitUsername.hint: Your GitHub login. Used in clone URLs as a hint for the system Git Credential Manager.
settings.github.token.hint: Created at github.com → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens. Classic or fine-grained both work; scope 'repo' is enough.
settings.gitlab.namespace.hint: GitLab group full-path (e.g. 'my-team' or 'my-team/sub-team') or your username. One source = one namespace; subgroups are not auto-included.
settings.gitlab.gitUsername.hint: Your GitLab login. Used in clone URLs as a hint for the system Git Credential Manager.
settings.gitlab.token.hint: Created at gitlab.com → User settings → Access tokens. Required scopes: read_api, read_repository.
settings.gitlab.baseUrl.hint: Self-hosted only. Full URL of your GitLab instance (e.g. https://gitlab.mycorp.com). Leave empty for gitlab.com.
settings.azure.organization.hint: Azure DevOps org slug (the part after dev.azure.com/). One source = one org; all projects and their repos are listed flat.
settings.azure.gitUsername.hint: Your Azure DevOps email or login. Used in clone URLs as a hint for the system Git Credential Manager.
settings.azure.token.hint: Created at dev.azure.com → User settings → Personal access tokens. Required scopes: Code: Read, Build: Read.
settings.azure.baseUrl.hint: Self-hosted only. Full URL of your Azure DevOps Server (e.g. https://devops.mycorp.com/tfs/DefaultCollection). Leave empty for dev.azure.com.
settings.bitbucket.title: Bitbucket
settings.bitbucket.description: Email, workspace, username and API token for the Cloud REST API.
settings.bitbucket.email.hint: Atlassian account email — same for Bitbucket and Jira.
settings.bitbucket.workspace.hint: The short ID from your Bitbucket URL (bitbucket.org/<workspace>/…).
settings.bitbucket.gitUsername.hint: Different from email. Used in clone URLs.
settings.bitbucket.token.hint: Created at id.atlassian.com → Security → API tokens.
settings.jira.title: Jira
settings.jira.description: Reuses your Atlassian email; needs its own API token.
settings.jira.token.hint: Use a classic token (no scopes). See guide for why.
settings.paths.title: Paths
settings.paths.description: Where projects clone, where SQL dumps live, how VS Code is launched.
settings.paths.dumpsRoot.hint: Where SQL dumps live; auto-detected by name pattern.
settings.database.title: Database
settings.database.description: Local MySQL connection — size detection, restore, drop/create.
settings.database.mysqlExecutable.notFou: not found — restore will be blocked
settings.database.mysqlExecutable.option: optional — only needed for restoring dumps
settings.database.psqlExecutable.notFoun: not found — restore will be blocked
settings.database.psqlExecutable.optiona: optional — only needed for restoring dumps. Point to psql.exe, pg_restore.exe, or the bin/ directory containing both.
settings.dotnet.title: .NET
settings.dotnet.description: Optional dotnet run arguments. Most users leave this empty.
settings.dotnet.runArgs.hint: Space-separated, passed to dotnet run after --
settings.presence.title: Presence
settings.presence.description: See which colleagues have WorkHelper open on the same network.
settings.presence.privacy: Off by default. Shares hostname, username, local IP and version with others on your network or Tailnet.
settings.appearance.title: Appearance
settings.appearance.description: Display preferences for this machine. Stored locally.
settings.appearance.autoRefresh.hint: Periodically fetches the Bitbucket list in the background
settings.appearance.searchHighlight: Highlight search matches in the projects table
settings.testReadsStored: Test reads stored credentials — Save first if you've changed fields above.
settings.guide.bitbucket.dialogDescripti: 4 steps to authenticate against Bitbucket Cloud. ~2 minutes.
settings.guide.github.dialogDescription: 3 steps to authenticate against GitHub via a Personal Access Token.
settings.guide.gitlab.dialogDescription: 3 steps to authenticate against GitLab Cloud or self-hosted via a Personal Access Token.
settings.guide.azure.dialogDescription: 3 steps to authenticate against Azure DevOps (Cloud or Server) via a Personal Access Token.
settings.guide.jira.dialogDescription: 3 steps; reuses your Atlassian email from the Bitbucket section.
settings.guide.paths.dialogDescription: Where projects clone, where dumps live, how VS Code is launched.
settings.guide.database.dialogDescriptio: Local MySQL connection used for size, restore, drop/create.
settings.guide.postgres.dialogDescriptio: PostgreSQL connection used for size, restore, drop/create.
settings.guide.dotnet.dialogDescription: Optional run arguments for dotnet run. Most users leave empty.
settings.guide.presence.dialogDescriptio: How to see colleagues running WorkHelper on your network.
settings.github.guide.title: GitHub setup
settings.gitlab.guide.title: GitLab setup
settings.azure.guide.title: Azure DevOps setup
settings.postgres.guide.title: PostgreSQL setup
